page: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv6 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, with a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. It is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware, though the instance is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as multi-core Geekbench testing. The AMD EPYC processor demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation with Clang and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, both scoring in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks reaches top-tier speeds of 7124.96 GB/sec, while larger uncached read and write operations fall into the top 25% bracket. For machine learning tasks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on medium-sized LLM models, though performance drops to average levels when processing larger 70B models.

The primary tradeoff of the F48als_v6 is its low memory density and the complete absence of local storage, which requires relying on external premium storage. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server maximizes compute density by dedicating physical cores without hyperthreading, offering high compute-per-resource value for CPU-bound workloads. This configuration is suitable for compute-heavy tasks such as software compilation, image processing, ray tracing, and medium-scale language model inference, but is less suited for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching.
description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the Falsv6 family, featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of memory, representing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and contains no local storage or GPU hardware. In benchmark evaluations, the server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and small-block memory bandwidth. However, its low memory allocation and lack of local storage present clear tradeoffs for memory-heavy applications. This instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, image processing, ray tracing, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB of RAM, and top-tier CPU benchmark performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is a compute-optimized server with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core CPU performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a low memory-to-CPU ratio for intensive processing tasks.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and software compilation speeds
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Suited for software compilation, image processing, and medium-scale LLM inference
